title: The Fellowship of the Ring
abstract: The first volume of The Lord of the Rings. The hobbit Frodo Baggins inherits a mysterious Ring from his uncle Bilbo. The wizard Gandalf reveals it to be the One Ring of the Dark Lord Sauron, and Frodo must leave the Shire on a perilous quest to destroy it.

title: The Two Towers
abstract: The second volume of The Lord of the Rings. The Fellowship has been broken at Amon Hen. Frodo and Sam journey alone towards Mordor guided by the treacherous Gollum, while Aragorn, Legolas and Gimli pursue the Uruk-hai who have captured Merry and Pippin.

title: Nineteen Eighty-Four
abstract: A chilling dystopian novel set in a totalitarian super-state called Oceania. Winston Smith, a low-ranking Party member, secretly harbours doubts about the regime and begins a forbidden love affair. A harrowing portrayal of surveillance, propaganda and the destruction of truth.

title: Animal Farm
abstract: A satirical allegorical novella in which farm animals overthrow their human farmer, only to find themselves under an even more oppressive regime led by the pigs. A pointed critique of Stalinist Soviet Russia wrapped in a deceptively simple fable.

title: Dune
abstract: Set in a feudal interstellar future, young Paul Atreides arrives on the desert planet Arrakis – the only source of the universe's most valuable substance. Betrayed and hunted, Paul must find his destiny among the Fremen people of the deep desert. Winner of the Hugo and Nebula Awards.

title: One Hundred Years of Solitude
abstract: The multi-generational saga of the Buendía family in the fictional town of Macondo. A landmark of magical realism, it weaves together the mythical and the mundane to tell the story of an entire continent across a century of solitude, love, war and obsession.
